title: SSH FIDO2 Resident Keys not working with podman-remote
body:
### Issue Description

It appears that `podman-remote` doesn't play nicely with FIDO2 resident keys.

If you don't specify an identity, you can actually get away with adding the connection and it even prompted my for my Yubikey's PIN and waited for touch verification.

However, running `podman-remote info` fails as described below.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

>[!NOTE]
>This is assuming that you already have a FIDO2 resident ssh key configured on a Yubikey or similar. See [Yubico's documentation](https://developers.yubico.com/SSH/Securing_SSH_with_FIDO2.html) for more details.

1. `podman-remote system connection add foo ssh://user@host`
2. `podman-remote info`


### Describe the results you received

Adding the connection worked:

```sh
podman-remote system connection add --log-level=trace arcadia ssh://chandler@arcadia
```

```sh
INFO[0000] podman-remote filtering at log level trace
DEBU[0000] Found SSH_AUTH_SOCK "/home/chandler/.ssh/agent/s.99PJi4TbRF.agent.4wtoTAaOon", ssh-agent signer enabled
DEBU[0000] SSH Agent Key SHA256:VDCaFRnE2qLOOyuBGx4VY9KNq+irL8IXgf4dsbWUA3A sk-ssh-ed25519@openssh.com
DEBU[0005] Shutting down engines
```

I was prompted for the PIN and it waited for touch, worked as expected, exit code 0.

The failure happened running `podman-remote info`:

```sh
podman-remote --log-level=trace info
```

```sh
INFO[0000] podman-remote filtering at log level trace
DEBU[0000] Called info.PersistentPreRunE(podman-remote --log-level=trace info)
DEBU[0000] ssh_config alias found: arcadia
DEBU[0000]   User: chandler
DEBU[0000]   Hostname: ssh://chandler@arcadia:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
DEBU[0000]   Port: 22
DEBU[0000]   IdentityFile: "/home/chandler/.ssh/id_ed25519_sk_rk"
DEBU[0000] Using Podman machine with `qemu` virtualization provider
OS: linux/amd64
provider: qemu
version: 5.8.2

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to read identity "/home/chandler/.ssh/id_ed25519_sk_rk": ssh: unhandled key type
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected to be prompted for the PIN and for this to work.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Running on x86_64 Arch Linux with podman installed via pacman. 

SSH client version: `OpenSSH_10.3p1, OpenSSL 3.6.2 7 Apr 2026`

### Additional information

Apologies if this should have been a feature request, was on the fence and leaned towards bug report as this is one of the few times I've run into compatibility issues with FIDO2 ssh keys.

comments.body: we use `golang.org/x/crypto/ssh` which does not support fido2.  see https://github.com/golang/go/issues/69904.  for historical perspective, see `vendor/golang.org/x/crypto/ssh/keys.go:1424` which switches on Keytype.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
